title: hearth.perf.ai_call v1
description: Emitted by hearth-brain's query router after each AI provider call completes (success or failure). Covers the full lifecycle: router selection, provider call, response. Enables latency tracking per provider and surfaces timeout/rate-limit patterns without log scraping. Source: hearth_brain::router::QueryRouter.

provider.description: Which AI provider handled this call.

model.description: Model identifier used for this call, e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514' or 'qwen2.5:3b'.

elapsed_ms.description: Wall-clock time from request start to first token (streaming) or full response (non-streaming).

success.description: Whether the provider returned a usable response. False covers HTTP errors, timeouts, and quality-gate failures.

failure_reason.description: Set when success=false. Helps distinguish transient failures (timeout, rate_limited) from configuration issues.

routed_from.description: Which provider was tried before this one (for fallthrough chains). 'initial' if this was the first attempt.

intent.description: Classified intent slug that triggered this call, e.g. 'knowledge_query' or 'device_control'. Optional.

prompt_tokens.description: Approximate prompt token count if known from the provider response.

completion_tokens.description: Approximate completion token count if known from the provider response.

ts.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp when the call completed.
